texto: My mom doesn't say anything. I don't say anything. Neither of us knows yet what you should say when rape victims blame themselves: 'It was not your fault.' It was not your fault, even if you were drunk, even if you were wearing a low-cut minidress, even if you were out walking alone at night, even if you were on a date with the rapist and kind of liked him but didn't want to have sex with him.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Joanna Connors sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “My mom doesn't say anything. I don't say anything. Neither of us knows yet what you should say wh…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, rape, recovery, útil em momentos de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
